name: Citron Developer Tooling Visual System
description: A dedicated cool-blue slate palette for development surfaces. Visually separate from warm Citron product UI so engineers immediately know they are in debug, admin, or tooling context.

productVsDev.product: Citron consumer app uses warm neutrals + citron (#c4a030) accent per tokens/primitive/color.tokens.json and semantic inkblot tokens.
productVsDev.devtools: Internal and dev-only UI uses blue primary (#2563EB) on dark slate (#020617 / #0B1220). Emerald (#10B981) marks positive dev signals only — it is not the product citron accent.
productVsDev.rule: If a user paying for the product could see it without a debug flag, do not use devtools tokens.

cssVariables.description: After npm run build, use semantic variables in dev-only CSS layers.

primitiveFallback.description: If you must reference primitives directly (e.g. codegen), use inkblot.color.devtools.* keys from dist/ai/inkblot-tokens-resolved.json

recommended: Vite / webpack dev-server overlay custom theme
Storybook theme (sidebar, preview chrome when wrapped)
Internal admin dashboards and feature-flag consoles
Local-only debug HUDs and FPS/profiler widgets
CLI dev subcommands when a distinct mode is desired (see system/cli.json truecolor devtools roles)
forbidden: Marketing site, onboarding, checkout, settings visible to all customers
Any surface gated only by URL obscurity — use product tokens if auth is weak

cssScoping.rule: Scope devtool styles under [data-inkblot-devtools='true'] on html or body, or a root class .inkblot-devtools, so product tokens remain default elsewhere.

focusAndStates.disabled: Text at 45% opacity of text.primary; no primary blue on disabled buttons — use surface + muted text.

contrast.rule: text.primary on background.app and surface meets WCAG AA for large and normal text; verify if you change hex.

pairings.mutedOnSurface: text.muted on surface is for secondary only — not long body

improvingThePalette.rule: Adjust only in tokens/primitive/devtools.palette.tokens.json; semantic layer follows automatically after build.

citronHarmony.description: This palette is complementary contrast to citron: cool vs warm. If unifying slightly, nudge blues toward slightly teal (#2563EB → #1D4ED8) rather than borrowing citron amber into dev chrome — keeps cognitive separation.
citronHarmony.doNot: Do not replace dev primary with #c4a030 — that collapses dev vs product distinction.

aiAgents.rule: When generating dev-only UI, load system/devtools.json and use devtools semantic CSS variables. When generating product UI, ignore devtools tokens and use standard semantic tokens.
